title: OpenAI API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: OpenAI project-scoped API key detected with sk-proj- prefix. OpenAI project-scoped API keys provide access to GPT models, DALL-E, Whisper, embeddings, and other OpenAI services with project-level isolation. Exposing these keys enables unauthorized API usage, incurring significant costs, accessing your AI models and data, and potential data exfiltration through prompt injection. Revoke this key immediately from platform.openai.com and use environment variables or secret management systems for secure storage.

OpenAI legacy API key detected with sk- prefix. OpenAI API keys grant full access to GPT language models, DALL-E image generation, Whisper speech recognition, embeddings, and fine-tuned models. Exposing these keys allows unauthorized users to make API calls at your expense, access proprietary model configurations, exfiltrate training data, and abuse AI services for malicious purposes. Revoke immediately and implement secure credential storage.

OpenAI API key detected in variable assignment. OpenAI API keys authenticate requests to GPT models, DALL-E, embeddings, and other AI services. Exposing these keys in code allows unauthorized users to consume your API quota, access fine-tuned models, retrieve conversation history, and incur substantial costs. Use secure credential management and rotate keys immediately if exposed.

OpenAI API key detected in Bearer authorization header. Bearer tokens with OpenAI API keys provide direct access to all OpenAI services including GPT models, image generation, and embeddings. Exposing these keys in authorization headers enables attackers to make unlimited API calls at your expense, access your AI models and training data, and abuse OpenAI services for malicious purposes. Store credentials securely and implement proper access controls.

Generic OpenAI API key detected in source code. OpenAI API keys authenticate access to GPT language models (GPT-4, GPT-3.5), DALL-E image generation, Whisper speech recognition, text embeddings, and fine-tuned models. Exposing these credentials enables unauthorized API usage, financial losses from API consumption, access to proprietary model configurations, and potential data exfiltration. Use environment variables or secret managers for secure credential storage and rotate keys immediately.
